sourceMovie.title: Time Bandits
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Time Bandits (1981) stuck because it commits to its family premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Terry Gilliam's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The family and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shrek Forever After
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Time Bandits clicked for you, Shrek Forever After (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Treasure Planet
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Time Bandits clicked for you, Treasure Planet (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Time Bandits?
faq.answer: Try Shrek Forever After, Treasure Planet, DuckTales: The Movie - Treasure of the Lost Lamp—each shares DNA with Time Bandits while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Time Bandits?
faq.answer: Time Bandits (1981) is best described as Family, Fantasy, Science Fiction, Adventure, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Time Bandits worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this family lane.
